title: build http://.../context.tar: Error: no FROM statement found
body:
The `bud-http-context-with-Dockerfile` test (others, too) is failing under podman-remote rootless. Reproducer (assumes a podman server running):
```console
$ BT=../buildah/tests
$ go build -o serve $BT/serve/serve.go 
$ portfile=$(mktemp)
$ ./serve $BT/bud/http-context 0 $portfile &  
[3] 1807608
$ port=$(<$portfile)
$ bin/podman-remote build -t foo http://0.0.0.0:$port/context.tar
Error: no FROM statement found
```
Only fails when **client** is rootless remote. Works perfectly fine when client is root. Works perfectly fine with podman (non-remote) rootless.

It's not the `./serve` process: running `sudo bin/podman-remote build ...`, against exactly the same server process set up above, works fine.

Running the server with `trace`, the only interesting log entry I see is:
```
DEBU[0019] buildStage(node.Children=[]*parser.Node{(*parser.Node)(0xc00064eb60), (*parser.Node)(0xc00064ee00)}) 
time="2023-03-14T13:28:07-06:00" level=trace msg="{
  "errorDetail":{
    "message":"no FROM statement found\n"
  },
  "error":"no FROM statement found\n"
}"
API=response X-Reference-Id=0xc000538000
```
(by "interesting" I mean "this does not show up on the root server log").

comments.body:
Not sure if it's related... but here's a similar remote-rootless-only failure with a one-line reproducer:
```console
$ bin/podman-remote  build -t foo github.com/projectatomic/nulecule-library
Error: context must be a directory: "/dev/shm/buildah488696279/nulecule-library-master"
```

For some reason and I do not know why we download and extract the tarball on the client just to tar it up again and send it on sever, instead we should just pass the remote url to the server and let it handle the download/extract thing which is much more efficient.

I am not sure where the bug is coming from but as rootless the extracted dir contains a Dockerfile which is just the tar:
```
file /var/tmp/buildah349887246/Dockerfile 
/var/tmp/buildah349887246/Dockerfile: POSIX tar archive (GNU)
```
while as root it is correctly extracted and just a plain text file.

Ok, the issues is that buildah falls back to copy it as flat file when chrootarchive.Untar() fails:
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/b5060e29537ec0283a948f59148a07f0c83d4e92/define/types.go#L252-L268

The chroot is failing with `creating mount namespace before pivot: operation not permitted` because remote does not join the podman user namespace.

This seems to go all the way back to https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/687/commits/c5d27989aa994eb3213c877e25c1fe628a46c2bc
This behaviour makes sense to me but we should likely check the file type and error out if the input is a tar archive.

As for fixing I think the easiest thing is to move this to the server side, as mentioned this will be much more efficient anyway and the server requires the userns so it should work there.
@flouthoc I have just hit this issue with a remote podman client. Was there any progress to move that logic on server side since the last update ?
